title: cuscuta gracilior var. kotschyi
intro: Cuscuta gracilior var. kotschyi, also known as Dodder, is a parasitic plant that grows on various host plants, obtaining its nutrients by attaching itself to the host's vascular system. Traditionally, Cuscuta gracilior var. kotschyi has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and providing relief from respiratory issues and fever. Cuscuta gracilior var. kotschyi can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
